newton-chronology-of-ancient-kingdoms.tx.author: Isaac Newton
newton-chronology-of-ancient-kingdoms.tx.note: Newton's attempt to re-date the ancient world against the biblical record — his own version of the project behind this site's Chronology feature. His conclusions are now dated, but the method (weighing sources, not asserting) is the same spirit.
newton-chronology-of-ancient-kingdoms.tx.title: The Chronology of Ancient Kingdoms Amended

newton-observations-daniel-apocalypse.tx.author: Isaac Newton
newton-observations-daniel-apocalypse.tx.note: Newton's historicist reading of the two great apocalyptic books — the same Daniel and Revelation this translation has begun. The manuscript in which he calculated the world would not end before the year 2060 is a separate unpublished paper (Yahuda MS 7.3g), not this book.
newton-observations-daniel-apocalypse.tx.title: Observations upon the Prophecies of Daniel, and the Apocalypse of St. John

newton-two-notable-corruptions-of-script.author: Isaac Newton
newton-two-notable-corruptions-of-script.note: Newton's textual criticism arguing that the Johannine Comma (1 John 5:7) and 1 Timothy 3:16 are later corruptions — the two passages the site's New Testament apparatus page already names as famously disputed. NB: this is OCR of a scanned reprint, so expect scanning artefacts; the 1733/1728 works above are clean Gutenberg transcriptions.
newton-two-notable-corruptions-of-script.title: An Historical Account of Two Notable Corruptions of Scripture
